title: Device Identification for Corporate-Managed Endpoints

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

dataSources: `ms:o365:management` device fields, `WinEventLog:Security` 4768 (Kerberos) with device claims if enabled
app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742)
spl:
index=o365 sourcetype=ms:o365:management earliest=-24h DeviceId=*
| stats latest(OperatingSystem) as os, dc(UserId) as users by DeviceId
| lookup corp_managed_devices.csv DeviceId OUTPUT owner, compliance_state
| where compliance_state!="compliant" OR isnull(compliance_state)
value: Correlates certificate- or MDM-based device IDs with authentication events for IA-3 device identification and compliance.
implementation: (1) Build `corp_managed_devices.csv` from Intune/JAMF export; (2) refresh nightly; (3) alert on unknown DeviceId with privileged users; (4) document attribute mapping in SSP.
visualization: Table (non-compliant devices), Pie chart (compliance mix), Single value (unknown devices)
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.process_name, Processes.user, Processes.dest | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
